revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神原神社 |画像 = [[Image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
神原神社本殿 |所在地 = 島根県雲南市加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|53|45|E|}} |祭神 = 大国主神 |社格 = 式内社
村社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = 11月9日 |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）とは[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘が刻まれた[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也」とある。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「神原神社」と記されている。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。復元した場合は29m×25mの[[方墳]]で、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最も古い時期に作られたもののひとつとされる前期の古墳であった。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (島根県)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に'''「景初三年」の銘が刻まれた三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた副葬品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] ==参考文献== *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:雲南市]] [[Category:出雲国]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

村社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = 11月9日 |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也」とある。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「神原神社」と記されている。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29m×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (島根県)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（239年）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] ==参考文献== *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:雲南市]] [[Category:出雲国]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也」とある。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「神原神社」と記されている。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29m×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (島根県)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（239年）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] ==参考文献== *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市]] [[Category:出雲国]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

神原神社本殿 |所在地 = 島根県雲南市加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|53|45|E|}} |祭神 = 大国主神 |社格 = 式内社
村社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = 11月9日 |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也」とある。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「神原神社」と記されている。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29m×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (島根県)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（239年）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] ==参考文献== *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市]] [[Category:出雲国]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

神原神社本殿 |所在地 = 島根県雲南市加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=神原神社|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大国主神 |社格 = 式内社
村社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = 11月9日 |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也」とある。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「神原神社」と記されている。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29[[メートル|m]]×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (雲南市)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（239年）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{デフォルトソート:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]] [[Category:出雲国|社かんはら]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

神原神社本殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=神原神社|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大国主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也」とある。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「神原神社」と記されている。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29[[メートル|m]]×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (雲南市)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（239年）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{デフォルトソート:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]] [[Category:雲南市の建築物]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

神原神社本殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=神原神社|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大国主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に見えるのが初見である。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}、[[宝永]]5年（1708年）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。また、『出雲国風土記』大原郡条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也（所造天下大神（[[大国主命]]）の宝が置かれた場所である）」とも記されている。[[延喜式神名帳]]には「神原神社」とある。[[慶長]]16年（1611年）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（1717年）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] [[ファイル:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|三角縁神獣鏡（複製）
{{small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29[[メートル|m]]×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 昭和47年（1972年）の[[赤川 (雲南市)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（239年）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』（[[明石書店]]、2006年） ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』（[[新人物往来社]]、2001年）ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{デフォルトソート:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]] [[Category:雲南市の建築物]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 神原神社 |画像 = [[Image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 神在祭

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kanbara Shrine |Image = [[Image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate Names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand Deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[ja:神原神社]]

Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand Deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:神原神社]]

The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:神原神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kanbara Shrine |Image = [[Image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate Names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand Deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[ja:神原神社]]

Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:神原神社]]

The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:神原神社]]

revision.comment: 画像変更。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神原神社 |画像 = [[Image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
神原神社本殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=神原神社|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大国主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に見えるのが初見である。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}、[[宝永]]5年（[[1708年]]）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。また、『出雲国風土記』大原郡条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也（所造天下大神（[[大国主命]]）の宝が置かれた場所である）」とも記されている。[[延喜式神名帳]]には「神原神社」とある。[[慶長]]16年（[[1611年]]）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（[[1717年]]）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] [[ファイル:神原神社古墳 三角縁神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|180px|right|三角縁神獣鏡
（国の[[重要文化財]]）
{{small|[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]展示。}}]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29[[メートル|m]]×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 [[昭和47年]]（[[1972年]]）の[[赤川 (雲南市)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（[[239年]]）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』（[[明石書店]]、2006年） ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』（[[新人物往来社]]、2001年）ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{デフォルトソート:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]] [[Category:雲南市の建築物]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kanbara Shrine |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate Names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:神原神社]]

Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:神原神社]]

revision.comment: fixing parameters en masse
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kanbara Shrine |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate Names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:神原神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:神原神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:神原神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:神原神社]]

ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:神原神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:神原神社]]

ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[ja:神原神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kanbara Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[ja:神原神社]]
