Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[ja:神原神社]]

{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[ja:神原神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Kanbara Shrine]][[Category:方墳]] [[ja:神原神社]]

The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient burial mound on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}} The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Alternate names = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}
