Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

revision.comment: /* 神原神社古墳 */ 画像追加。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神原神社 |画像 = [[Image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]]
神原神社本殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町神原1436 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=神原神社|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大国主神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 神宝大明神 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 神在祭
獅子舞 }} '''神原神社'''（かんばらじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[雲南市]]加茂町にある神社である。旧社地にあった[[古墳]]から「[[景初]]三年」の銘を有する[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 祭神 == [[大国主|大国主神]]、[[イワツツノオ|磐筒男命]]、磐筒女命を祀る。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[大原郡]]条の神原郷の項に見えるのが初見である。当初は大国主神のみを祀ったものと見られるが{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}、[[宝永]]5年（[[1708年]]）に三刀屋天満宮宮司の広沢漆拾がまとめた『神原神社縁起』では祭神が磐筒男命・磐筒女命とされている。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』には「神原社」と記され、[[神祇官]]に所属しているとある。また、『出雲国風土記』大原郡条の神原郷の項に「所造天下大神之御財 積置給處也（所造天下大神（[[大国主命]]）の宝が置かれた場所である）」とも記されている。[[延喜式神名帳]]には「神原神社」とある。[[慶長]]16年（[[1611年]]）の棟札には「神原松井大明神」、前述の『神原神社縁起』では「神原神社松井大明神」「松井神寶大神宮」、[[享保]]2年（[[1717年]]）に出された『雲陽誌』には「神寶明神」と記されている。 [[明治4年]]12月に村社に列せられた。現在の社殿は後述の赤川の改修工事に伴い昭和47年（1972年）に遷移されたものである。 == 神原神社古墳 == [[画像:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|神原神社古墳の石室（復元）]] [[ファイル:神原神社古墳 三角縁神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|180px|right|三角縁神獣鏡
（国の[[重要文化財]]）
{{small|[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]展示。}}]] 旧社地は古墳の上にあった。この古墳は[[方墳]]で、復元した場合の規模は29[[メートル|m]]×25m、高さは5m程と推定される。島根県では最古に属する前期古墳である。 [[昭和47年]]（[[1972年]]）の[[赤川 (雲南市)|赤川]]（[[斐伊川]]水系）の改修工事で社地が新堤防域に組み込まれるために神社を南西に50mほど遷移することになり、その際に古墳の発掘調査が行われた。竪穴式石室からの出土品の中に[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の'''「景初三年」'''（[[239年]]）の銘が鋳出された'''三角縁神獣鏡'''があった。この銅鏡を含めた出土品は一括して国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。出土品は国（文化庁）所有で、[[島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館]]に保管。石室は移築された社殿の東側に復元されていて自由に見学できる。 神原神社古墳 素環頭大刀.JPG|素環頭大刀（中央）
{{small|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館展示。}} 神原神社古墳 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品
{{small|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館展示。}} == 祭礼 == 11月9日に行われる例祭には[[島根県指定文化財一覧|県指定無形民俗文化財]]の獅子舞[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/mukeiminnzoku.html]が奉納される。[[神無月|神在祭]]の期間には神事が行われる。 == 近郊の史跡 == *[[加茂岩倉遺跡]] *[[荒神谷遺跡]] == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』（[[明石書店]]、2006年） ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』（[[新人物往来社]]、2001年）ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 神原神社古墳（雲南市のサイト）] *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 出雲神原神社古墳出土品 文化遺産オンライン] {{デフォルトソート:かんはらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]] [[Category:雲南市の建築物]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft|12=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=draft|12=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanbara Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]]

It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dating to the early period. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Former village shrines]] to [[Category:Son-sha]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Unnan City|ja|雲南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the {{ill|Hii River system|ja|斐伊川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]]

Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|ja|島根県立古代出雲歴史博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|qq=}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamo Iwakura Site|ja|加茂岩倉遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Iwakura Site|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|ja|荒神谷遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjindani Site|qq=}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]]

ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]]

Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[en:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:雲南市の建築物]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]]

However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Unnan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]]

{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanbara Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]]
