revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]]

These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:雲南市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:雲南の Municipal History]]

Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =
Kanbara Shrine Main Hall |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46.5|N|132|53|44.8|E|region:JP-32|name=Kanbara Shrine|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]]

Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "prefecture = {{ill|" to "location = {{ill|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|qid=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]]

Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|qid=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]]

The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|qid=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|qid=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[sn:Kanbara Shrine]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[commons:" to "[[sn:commons:"
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|qid=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|qq=|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}

Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|qq=|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|qq=|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}
