[[commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[ja:神原神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. [[commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[ja:神原神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|ja|大原郡|ko|오하라군|zh|大原郡|lt=Ōhara district|WD=Q865650}} || {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|ja|神原神社|commons|Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)|WD=Q11588802}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|35.346449|132.895821|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182946 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[ja:神原神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|ja|大原郡|ko|오하라군|zh|大原郡|lt=Ōhara district|WD=Q865650}} || {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|ja|神原神社|commons|Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)|WD=Q11588802}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|35.346449|132.895821|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182946 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]][[ja:神原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588802]] Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, [[:en:Unnan|Unnan City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]]. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the [[:en:Hii River|Hii River system]]. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]].}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], housed at the [[:en:Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum]]. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * [[:en:Kamoiwakura Site|Kamo Iwakura Site]] * [[:en:Kōjindani Site|Kōjindani Site]] == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] [[ja:神原神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|ja|大原郡|ko|오하라군|zh|大原郡|lt=Ōhara district|WD=Q865650}} || {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|ja|神原神社|commons|Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)|WD=Q11588802}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|35.346449|132.895821|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182946 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Kanbara-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|ja|大原郡|ko|오하라군|zh|大原郡|lt=Ōhara district|WD=Q865650}} || {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|ja|神原神社|commons|Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)|WD=Q11588802}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|35.346449|132.895821|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182946 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]] [[da:d:Q11588802]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|ja|大原郡|ko|오하라군|zh|大原郡|lt=Ōhara district|WD=Q865650}} || {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|ja|神原神社|commons|Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)|WD=Q11588802}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|35.346449|132.895821|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182946 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]] [[da:d:Q11588802]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri}} |alternate_name = Kanpō Daimyōjin |Festival = November 9 |Rituals = Kamiari Festival
Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kanbara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kambarajinjahonden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City|1=Unnan}}, Kamochō Kanbara 1436 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34625|N|132.895778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)

Shishi-mai (Lion Dance) |location = {{ill|Unnan|WD=Q877935|1=Unnan}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (神原神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamochō|ja|加茂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamochō|1=kamochou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398866}}, {{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan City|1=Unnan}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is known for the discovery of a {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} with the inscription "Jingchu 3rd Year" during the excavation of an ancient Kofun on its former grounds. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Iwatsutsunoo|ja|磐筒男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsutsunoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. The earliest record appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the entry for Kanbara-go in Ōhara District. Initially, it is believed that only Ōkuninushi was worshipped {{why|date=2022}}. However, the 1708 document ''Kanbara Shrine Engi'' compiled by Hirozawa Urushihiro, the priest of {{ill|Mito Tenmangū|ja|三刀屋天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, lists the deities as Iwatsutsunoo and Iwatsutsume-no-Mikoto. == History == The ''{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' refers to the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine," stating that it belonged to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}}. The record also describes Kanbara-go as "the place where the treasures of the Heavenly Grand deity (Ōkuninushi) are stored." The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} lists the shrine as "Kanbara Shrine." Various names have appeared over time, including "Kanbara Matsui Daimyōjin" (1611) on a ridgepole inscription, "Kanbara Shrine Matsui Daimyōjin" in the ''Kanbara Shrine Engi,'' and "Shinpo Myōjin" in the 1717 ''Un'yōshi.'' In December 1871, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. The current structure was relocated in 1972 due to flood control work on the {{ill|Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja|赤川 (雲南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagawa River (Unnan City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the {{ill|Hii River|en|Hii River|WD=Q11500714|lt=Hii River system|1=Hii River}}. == Kanbara Shrine Kofun == [[File:kambarajinjakofunsekisitsu.JPG|thumb|right|Reconstructed stone chamber of Kanbara Shrine Kofun]] [[File:神原神社古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|Replica of a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|WD=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History|1=National Museum of Japanese History}}.}}]] The shrine's former grounds were situated atop an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]). The mound is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, estimated to measure 29 meters by 25 meters and approximately 5 meters in height. It is among the oldest kofun in {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, dating to the early period. During flood control work in 1972, the shrine was moved approximately 50 meters southwest, and the kofun was excavated. Artifacts found in the [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chamber]] included a "Jingchu 3rd Year" (239 CE) {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, identified as a relic of the {{ill|Three Kingdoms period|ja|三国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Kingdoms period|1=Three Kingdoms period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354714}}. These artifacts are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}, housed at the {{ill|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|en|Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo|WD=Q4802099|lt=Shimane Prefectural Ancient Izumo History Museum|1=Shimane Museum of Ancient Izumo}}. The stone chamber has been reconstructed east of the relocated shrine and is open for public viewing. == Festivals == The annual festival held on November 9 includes a lion dance, designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Shimane Prefecture|1=Q11476462|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476462}}. Rituals are also conducted during the {{ill|Kamiari Festival|ja|神無月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiari Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kannazuki|WD=Q6364021}} period. == Nearby Historic Sites == * {{ill|Kamoiwakura Site|en|Kamoiwakura Site|WD=Q11398844|lt=Kamo Iwakura Site|1=Kamoiwakura Site}} * {{ill|Kōjindani Site|en|Kōjindani Site|WD=Q11618840|lt=Kōjindani Site|1=Kōjindani Site}} == References == * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on Izumo no Kuni Fudoki'' (Akashi Shoten, 2006). ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takioto, ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Izumo'' (Shinjinbutsu Oraisha, 2001). ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == External Links == * [http://www.city.unnan.shimane.jp/cgi-bin/odb-get.exe?WIT_template=AC020000&WIT_oid=icityv2::Contents::2081 Kanbara Shrine Kofun (Unnan City website)] * [http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=103587 Cultural Heritage Online: Kanbara Shrine Kofun Artifacts] {{translated page|ja|神原神社|version=104494616|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:kambarajinjahonden.JPG|250px]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kanbara Shrine (Q11588802)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ohara District, Shimane|en|Ohara District, Shimane|ja|大原郡|ko|오하라군|zh|大原郡|lt=Ōhara district|WD=Q865650|1=Ohara District, Shimane}} || {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|ja|神原神社|commons|Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)|WD=Q11588802|1=Kanbara Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.346449|132.895821|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182946 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11588802}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]] [[da:d:Q11588802]] [[ja:神原神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kanbara-jinja (Unnan)]] [[Category:Buildings in Unnan, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Municipal History of Unnan]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]
